market_note: Combined USDT + USDC market cap now ~$260B, roughly 3x its 2023 level, as the July 18 GENIUS Act compliance deadline approaches.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act final rules due July 18
stablecoin_items.summary: Six US regulators (OCC, FDIC, NCUA, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC) must finalize stablecoin frameworks by July 18, 2026, one year after the GENIUS Act was signed. Rules include a $5M issuer capital floor and no deposit insurance for token holders.

stablecoin_items.headline: 1:1 reserve and licensing mandate tightens the field
stablecoin_items.summary: Every issuer must hold 1:1 reserves in cash or short-term Treasuries and submit to audits. Circle and Coinbase can absorb compliance costs; smaller issuers may be forced to exit or partner up.

stablecoin_items.headline: Banks moving into the $323B stablecoin market
stablecoin_items.summary: Traditional banks are entering stablecoin issuance and tokenized deposits as the market matures, competing directly with Circle's USDC and Tether's USDT for payment rails share.

hack_items.headline: Humanity Protocol hack: $31M lost
hack_items.summary: Accounted for over 40% of June's total crypto losses. Attackers moved stolen funds across Bitcoin, Solana, Hyperliquid, and BNB Chain to obscure the trail.

hack_items.headline: Syscoin Bridge exploit: $10M drained
hack_items.summary: Second-largest incident of the month, continuing the pattern of cross-chain bridges as a top attack surface for DeFi protocols.

hack_items.headline: June losses hit $75.87M across 40 incidents
hack_items.summary: Roughly 40% of cumulative 2026 losses trace back to stolen or compromised private keys rather than smart contract bugs, per PeckShield's monthly tally.
